last_result.result: EasyCrypt accepted this read-only probe. The committed proof state was not changed; `goal_after_probe` shows the goal that would be visible if this tactic were committed.
last_result.effect: This is a read-only probe; it asks EasyCrypt what would happen without changing the committed proof state.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state was not changed.

probe_preview.effect: This preview is speculative only; the committed EasyCrypt proof state was not changed.

lines: forall (result_L result_R : bool) (lc_L : ciphertext list) (gs_L : globS)
(result_L = result_R /\ lc_L = lc_R) /\ gs_L = m_R => result_L = result_R

last_result.message: This structural transition probe was accepted.

structural_transition.decision: Commit this exact accepted tactic only if you want to enter the pRHL mid-surgery phase.
structural_transition.after_commit: The next view should expose the real pRHL mid-surgery workbench for the updated frontier.
structural_transition.if_wrong_after_commit: Use `undo_last_step` or `undo_to_checkpoint` if the committed route is wrong.

last_result.question: Do you want to enter the pRHL mid-surgery workbench?
last_result.guidance: If yes, commit this exact tactic and read the real post-commit workbench. If no, choose another probe or inspect the current state. Do not solve the speculative preview in your mental model.
last_result.if_route_wrong: Use `undo_last_step` or `undo_to_checkpoint` if the committed route is wrong.

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

moves.title: Read-only tactic probe
moves.applicability: This tactic-shaped option should be checked with a read-only probe before committing it.
moves.effect: This is a read-only probe; it asks EasyCrypt what would happen without changing the committed proof state.
moves.derivation: read-only probe surfaced from proof-state analysis
moves.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

probe_alternatives.how_to_use: This tactic was accepted by a read-only probe on the current proof state. The transition record describes the checked effect and any visible limitations.

structural_transition.decision: Commit this exact accepted tactic only if you want to enter the pRHL mid-surgery phase.
structural_transition.after_commit: The next view should expose the real pRHL mid-surgery workbench for the updated frontier.
structural_transition.if_wrong_after_commit: Use `undo_last_step` or `undo_to_checkpoint` if the committed route is wrong.

checks: A runnable seq candidate is not evidence that the invariant is strong enough.

selected_handles.title: Concrete tactic candidate
selected_handles.why_relevant: Daemon probe accepted this tactic and it would leave 4 subgoal(s); commit it if that is the intended decomposition.
selected_handles.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

selected_handles.title: Named-call context
selected_handles.why_relevant: program-difference analysis found this tactic while comparing the current programs/probability expressions. Unverified call/ecall items are structural hints only; proof-state analysis or a daemon probe must establish frontier readiness before they become runnable.
selected_handles.runnable_status: Not established as runnable from this panel; use the current frontier and call-site context before deciding whether to probe it.
missing_input: Concrete arguments required by the lemma or call form.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
The current cut or frontier context may expose a call route.
ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.
ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations

ask_manager_for.use_when: The visible cut may depend on LHS/RHS statement alignment or missing live facts.
ask_manager_for.returns: swap/alignment context for the visible pRHL frontier

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid form for call, seq, while, rnd, or rewrite.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: The visible cut/frontier may need indexed `sp i j` before branch tactics.
Mid-proof pRHL suffix surgery may need indexed `wp`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix-alignment traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp`, `wp`, or `sim`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.
A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken to a smaller relation before `sim`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `conseq` forms and pRHL memory-annotation traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: One side may have an extra sample or need an explicit sample coupling.
A known statement-order mismatch may need an eager/lazy transformation.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `eager` forms and when to prefer smaller surgery

lookup_candidates.use_when: Resolve exact signature for `UFCMA_genCC` only when this handle route is being tested.
